proposed_hypotheses.text: At least some binary features in the cohort are associated with mean pfs_months, with a t-test detecting differences in means between feature=1 and feature=0 patients.
feature_056 = 1 is associated with shorter mean pfs_months than feature_056 = 0.
feature_042 = 1 is associated with longer mean pfs_months than feature_042 = 0 (suggesting feature_042 may be a beneficial treatment).
feature_048 = 1 is associated with shorter mean pfs_months than feature_048 = 0.
feature_111 = 1 is associated with longer mean pfs_months than feature_111 = 0 (suggesting feature_111 may be a favorable biomarker).
feature_015 = 1 is associated with shorter mean pfs_months than feature_015 = 0.
feature_040 = 1 is associated with shorter mean pfs_months than feature_040 = 0.
feature_039 = 1 is associated with longer mean pfs_months than feature_039 = 0.

analyses.result_summary: feature_056=1 mean PFS 3.617 mo vs feature_056=0 mean PFS 5.153 mo (Welch t-test). Strongly negative prognostic.
feature_042=1 mean PFS 5.408 mo vs feature_042=0 mean PFS 4.310 mo. Largest positive marginal effect among binary features.
feature_048=1 mean PFS 3.750 mo vs feature_048=0 mean PFS 4.800 mo.
feature_111=1 mean PFS 4.865 mo vs feature_111=0 mean PFS 4.297 mo.
feature_015=1 mean PFS 4.328 mo vs feature_015=0 mean PFS 4.887 mo.
feature_040=1 mean PFS 4.317 mo vs feature_040=0 mean PFS 4.776 mo.
feature_039=1 mean PFS 4.824 mo vs feature_039=0 mean PFS 4.462 mo.

proposed_hypotheses.text: Among continuous features, at least one is strongly correlated (Pearson |r| > 0.10) with pfs_months.
feature_080 (range 30-90, mean ~65, plausibly age in years) is positively correlated with pfs_months.
feature_067 (continuous, range 0-25) is negatively correlated with pfs_months (consistent with an unfavorable lab marker).
feature_019 (continuous, range 1.5-5.5) is positively correlated with pfs_months (consistent with a favorable lab such as serum albumin).
feature_101 (continuous, range 1-100, mean ~15.5) is negatively correlated with pfs_months (consistent with a tumor-burden or lab-derangement marker).

analyses.result_summary: feature_080 has Pearson r = +0.7015 with pfs_months (Spearman +0.7264); largest correlation observed. Each unit increase in feature_080 is associated with substantially longer PFS.
feature_067 has Pearson r = -0.1155 with pfs_months. Negative prognostic continuous marker.
feature_019 has Pearson r = +0.1002 with pfs_months. Modest favorable association.
feature_101 has Pearson r = -0.0914 with pfs_months. Modest unfavorable association.

proposed_hypotheses.text: Among the seven multi-level integer features, at least one has an ordinal dose-response relationship with pfs_months.
feature_063 (3 levels: 0/1/2) shows a monotonic decrease in mean pfs_months as level increases (consistent with a stage- or burden-of-disease grading).
feature_021, feature_024, feature_016, feature_127, feature_001, and feature_073 do NOT show meaningful associations with pfs_months (they may be record-counting or codes lacking prognostic content).

analyses.result_summary: feature_063 shows strong monotonic decrease in mean PFS: 5.640 (n=17592) -> 4.444 (n=24971) -> 3.289 (n=7437). Spearman r=-0.334, ANOVA p effectively 0.

ANOVA p-values all > 0.27 for feature_021 (p=0.71), feature_024 (p=0.59), feature_016 (p=0.38), feature_127 (p=0.64), feature_001 (p=0.83), feature_073 (p=0.27). None of these multi-level integer features carry meaningful PFS information.

proposed_hypotheses.text: Mean pfs_months differs by self-reported race (feature_011: white, hispanic, black, asian, other), reflecting potential disparities.
Mean pfs_months differs by insurance status (feature_089: private, medicare, medicaid, uninsured), reflecting potential socioeconomic disparities.
Black patients have shorter pfs_months than white patients (the prevailing direction of US oncology disparities literature).
Medicaid or uninsured patients have shorter pfs_months than privately insured patients.

analyses.result_summary: ANOVA across 5 race groups p=0.494. Means: white 4.679, hispanic 4.707, black 4.717, asian 4.735, other 4.762. Pairwise vs white: black diff +0.037 (p=0.292), hispanic diff +0.028 (p=0.375), asian diff +0.056 (p=0.239), other diff +0.082 (p=0.298). No race disparity in pfs_months detected, with all observed differences <0.1 month.

ANOVA across 4 insurance groups p=0.818. Means: private 4.685, medicare 4.692, medicaid 4.706, uninsured 4.733. Pairwise vs private: medicare +0.007 (p=0.774), medicaid +0.021 (p=0.537), uninsured +0.048 (p=0.411). No insurance disparity in pfs_months detected.

proposed_hypotheses.text: feature_042 (the binary feature with the largest positive marginal effect on PFS) interacts with at least one other binary feature, indicating it is a treatment whose efficacy depends on a biomarker.
The interaction term between feature_042 and feature_111 in an OLS model on pfs_months is positive (feature_042's benefit is larger when feature_111=1).
The interaction term between feature_042 and feature_015 is negative (feature_042's benefit is abolished when feature_015=1).
The interaction term between feature_042 and feature_040 is negative (feature_042's benefit is abolished when feature_040=1).

analyses.result_summary: feature_042:feature_111 interaction beta = +1.594, p ~ 9e-229. feature_042 main effect (when feature_111=0) is -0.017 (NS). When feature_111=1 the treatment effect is +1.577 (p<1e-300).
feature_042:feature_015 interaction beta = -1.623, p ~ 4e-255. When feature_015=0, feature_042 effect = +1.66; when feature_015=1, effect = +0.04 (NS). feature_015=1 abolishes treatment benefit.
feature_042:feature_040 interaction beta = -1.364, p ~ 2e-118. When feature_040=0, feature_042 effect = +1.35; when feature_040=1, effect = -0.01 (NS). feature_040=1 abolishes treatment benefit.

proposed_hypotheses.text: Patients defined as 'biomarker-eligible' (feature_111=1 AND feature_015=0 AND feature_040=0) experience a large mean PFS gain from feature_042, while patients who do not meet all three criteria experience no benefit.
The interaction term feature_042 x responder (responder = composite of the three biomarkers) is large and positive in OLS on pfs_months.
feature_039 has no independent treatment effect once feature_111 status is controlled (its marginal +0.36 month effect is confounded by being correlated with feature_111).

analyses.code: df['responder']=((df['feature_111']==1)&(df['feature_015']==0)&(df['feature_040']==0)).astype(int); compare feature_042 means within responder strata
analyses.result_summary: Responder=1 (n=18681): feature_042 effect = +2.908 mo (treated 7.21 vs untreated 4.30, p~0). Responder=0 (n=31319): feature_042 effect = +0.003 mo (NS, p=0.91). Treatment benefit is entirely confined to the biomarker-defined subgroup.

feature_042:responder interaction beta = +2.905 (SE 0.045, p<1e-300). main feature_042 = +0.003 (NS), main responder = -0.015 (NS). The benefit of feature_042 is operationalized entirely through this composite biomarker.

analyses.code: feature_039 stratified within feature_111 x feature_015 cells
analyses.result_summary: phi(feature_039, feature_111) = 0.669 (highly correlated). Stratified within (feature_111, feature_015) cells, feature_039 has no independent effect: p>0.08 in every cell, with all stratified differences |<= 0.15 mo|.

proposed_hypotheses.text: feature_080 (age-like) does not modify the magnitude of the feature_042 treatment effect (treatment benefit is uniform across ages).
feature_101 (the negatively-prognostic continuous marker) significantly modifies the feature_042 treatment effect: higher feature_101 is associated with smaller (or absent) treatment benefit.
feature_067 and feature_019 modify the feature_042 effect only modestly or not at all.

analyses.code: smf.ols('pfs_months ~ feature_042 * feature_080 + responder + feature_042:responder', df).fit()
analyses.result_summary: feature_042:feature_080 interaction beta = -0.0020 (p=0.16). Treatment effect is uniform across the 30-90 range of feature_080.

analyses.code: smf.ols('pfs_months ~ feature_042 * feature_101 + responder + feature_042:responder', df).fit()
analyses.result_summary: feature_042:feature_101 interaction beta = -0.0517 (p ~ 2e-192). Each unit higher feature_101 reduces feature_042's treatment effect by 0.052 months.

analyses.code: smf.ols('pfs_months ~ feature_042 * feature_067 + responder + feature_042:responder', df).fit()
analyses.result_summary: feature_042:feature_067 interaction beta = +0.0138 (p=0.013, modest); feature_042:feature_019 beta = -0.028 (p=0.52, NS). Effects of feature_067 and feature_019 on the treatment-effect magnitude are small or null.

proposed_hypotheses.text: Within biomarker-eligible patients, the feature_042 treatment effect declines monotonically across quintiles of feature_101, becoming non-significant in the upper quintiles.
Among non-eligible patients, feature_042 has no benefit at any level of feature_101.

analyses.code: stratify responders by qcut(feature_101,5) and compute treated-vs-untreated diff per quintile
analyses.result_summary: In responders (n=18681): treatment effect by feature_101 quintile is +4.896 mo (q0: 1-6.7), +5.025 (q1: 6.8-10.2), +4.416 (q2: 10.3-14.6), +0.050 (q3: 14.7-22.0, NS), -0.037 (q4: 22.1-100.0, NS). Effect is large and significant only when feature_101 is below ~15.

analyses.code: stratify non-responders by qcut(feature_101,5) and compute treated-vs-untreated diff per quintile
analyses.result_summary: In non-responders (n=31319): treatment effect across all 5 feature_101 quintiles ranges from -0.057 to +0.064 months, p>0.24 in every stratum. No level of feature_101 produces benefit when biomarker eligibility is absent.

proposed_hypotheses.text: Receipt of feature_042 among biomarker-eligible (responder=1) patients is independent of race (feature_011) and insurance (feature_089).
Among biomarker-eligible patients who receive feature_042, the magnitude of the PFS benefit is similar across race groups.
Within responders the feature_042 effect is consistent across all three feature_063 (stage-like) levels.

analyses.code: chi2_contingency(pd.crosstab(sub['feature_011'], sub['feature_042'])) ; same for feature_089 within responders
analyses.result_summary: Treatment receipt P(feature_042=1) within responders: white 0.353, black 0.343, hispanic 0.357, asian 0.346, other 0.361 (chi-square p=0.846); medicaid 0.343, private 0.347, medicare 0.356, uninsured 0.378 (chi-square p=0.210). No disparity in treatment receipt.

analyses.code: for race in groups: stratified t-test of feature_042 within (responder=1, race=race)
analyses.result_summary: Treatment effect within responders is uniform across races: white +2.894 mo, black +2.895 mo, hispanic +2.957 mo, asian +2.981 mo, other +2.872 mo (all p<1e-15).

analyses.code: for s in [0,1,2]: t-test feature_042 within (responder=1, feature_063=s)
analyses.result_summary: Within responders, feature_042 effect across feature_063 strata: stage 0: +2.915 mo, stage 1: +2.919 mo, stage 2: +2.967 mo (all p<<0.0001). Treatment benefit is invariant to disease level.

proposed_hypotheses.text: An OLS model containing main effects for feature_080, feature_063, feature_056, feature_048, feature_042, feature_111, feature_015, feature_040, feature_067, feature_019, feature_101 plus the four key interactions (feature_042:feature_111, feature_042:feature_015, feature_042:feature_040, feature_042:feature_101) explains the bulk of variance in pfs_months (R^2 > 0.80).
After adjustment for the prognostic and treatment-biomarker model, race (feature_011) and insurance (feature_089) coefficients remain near zero with non-significant p-values.
feature_048 acts as an independent negative prognostic factor, with similar magnitude across all three feature_063 strata.
feature_056 acts as an independent negative prognostic factor, with similar magnitude across all three feature_063 strata.

analyses.code: smf.ols('pfs_months ~ feature_080 + feature_063 + feature_056 + feature_048 + feature_042 + feature_111 + feature_015 + feature_040 + feature_042:feature_111 + feature_042:feature_015 + feature_042:feature_040 + feature_042:feature_101 + feature_067 + feature_019 + feature_101', df).fit()
analyses.result_summary: Multivariable OLS R^2 = 0.8577 (residual SD 0.94 mo vs raw 2.50 mo). All coefficients in the prognostic block significant. feature_080 +0.176/unit, feature_063 -1.176/level, feature_056 -1.548, feature_048 -0.967, feature_042 main +1.665 (when biomarkers absent and feature_101=0), feature_042:feature_111 +1.531, feature_042:feature_015 -1.643, feature_042:feature_040 -1.325, feature_042:feature_101 -0.053, feature_067 -0.076/unit, feature_019 +0.476/unit.

analyses.code: same OLS with C(feature_011) + C(feature_089) added
analyses.result_summary: After full prognostic + treatment adjustment, every race coefficient has |beta| < 0.05 mo and p > 0.19; every insurance coefficient has |beta| < 0.01 mo and p > 0.62. Race and insurance contribute no detectable residual signal to PFS.
feature_048 effect: stage 0 -1.056 (p~1e-69), stage 1 -1.078 (p~3e-101), stage 2 -0.873 (p~7e-26). Independent and roughly constant across stage levels.
feature_056 effect: stage 0 -1.599 (p~0), stage 1 -1.506 (p~0), stage 2 -1.482 (p~1e-158). Independent and roughly constant across stage levels.
